codeReview.summary: The fix commit materially improves VAL-EXEC-012 by adding assertion-tagged run-journal sequence/replay tests in BurnBarRunServiceTests, but it does not close the other required rerun blockers. VAL-EXEC-001 still lacks failed/cancelled terminal-phase mapping assertions, and explicit VAL-EXEC-003 tagged provenance coverage is still missing.

issues.description: VAL-EXEC-001 remains incomplete: `testVAL_EXEC_001_RunPhaseToPacketStatusMappingIsDeterministic` still validates only `.completed`, with no assertion coverage for `.failed` or `.cancelled` terminal phase→packet-status mappings required by the rerun scope.

Explicit VAL-EXEC-003 tagged provenance coverage was not added. Provenance assertions remain in untagged `testMissionDispatchLinksRealRunAndSyncsResultProvenance`, and no `VAL_EXEC_003`-tagged test exists.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `runtime-orchestration-worker` validation command guidance is out of sync with canonical service commands, which likely contributed to procedural drift in this rerun.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/runtime-orchestration-worker/SKILL.md` lines 22-24 specify `OpenBurnBar*` filters, while `.factory/services.yaml` canonical commands use `BurnBar*` filters. The fix handoff reports `swift test --package-path OpenBurnBarDaemon --filter VAL_EXEC` and full-suite execution instead of the skill's listed focused commands.

summary: Review failed: the rerun fixes VAL-EXEC-012 evidence quality, but does not implement the required failed/cancelled VAL-EXEC-001 mapping assertions or explicit VAL-EXEC-003 tagged provenance coverage.
